comments.body: Spring Cloud efforts to set the hostname are best effort. If it can't set it, you'll need to set it in properties
I have fixed it by removing VirtualBox from my local machine.

As it’s currently written, your answer is unclear. Please edit to add additional details that will help others understand how this addresses the question asked. You can find more information on how to write good answers in the help center.

answers.body: You should uinstall any program virtualization por example Oracle VirtualBox, VmWare WorkStation Player

I had the similar problem on my local environment and solved by the config below.

application-local.properties:

spring.cloud.inetutils.preferred-networks=127.0.0.1

or

bootstrap.yml:

spring:
  config:
    activate:
    on-profile: local
  cloud:
    inetutils:
      preferred-networks: 127.0.0.1

You can also put list of IP addresses as comma seperated.

If you’re just running a single Eureka server on your machine, those registered-replicas fields being empty is totally normal.
Eureka only shows replicas when it’s part of a cluster, and you don’t have a cluster on localhost, so it literally has nothing to list.

The cannot determine hostname logs happen because windows has a zoo of virtual adapters and Eureka can’t pick one, but it doesn’t break anything for a standalone server..

If you really want to force an interface, you can set :

eureka.instance.hostname=localhost

or pick a real IP, but honestly on a single-node local setup it doesn’t change much.
The empty replica fields aren’t an error, they just mean " you’re not running a cluster ".

title: spring eureka - Cannot determine local hostname
body:
I am trying to deploy Spring Eureka server on my local machine. Unfortunately, the Eureka application cannot determine local hostname, through this values registered-replicas and unavaiable-replicas are empty.

How I can force spring application to choose one network interface?

Local Eureka application

pom file

...
    <properties>
        <spring-cloud.version>2021.0.5</spring-cloud.version>
    </properties>
    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-cloud-starter-netflix-eureka-client</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-cloud-starter-netflix-eureka-server</artifactId>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>
...

EurekApplication.java

@SpringBootApplication
@EnableEurekaServer
public class EurekaApplication {

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        SpringApplication.run(EurekaApplication.class, args);
    }
}

application.properties

server.port=8761

eureka.client.register-with-eureka=false
eureka.client.fetch-registry=false
logging.level.com.netflix.eureka=OFF
logging.level.com.netflix.discovery=OFF
logging.level.org.springframework.cloud.commons.util.InetUtils= trace

logs from console
